titleStmt.title: Letter from Andrew Philip Magill to Peter Burrowes Shiel, 29 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Andrew Philip Magill

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Andrew Philip Magill (1871-1941). Magill was a civil servant and held the position of private secretary for successive chief secretaries of Ireland until his transfer to the Northern Irish Civil Service in 1921. The letter was sent to Peter Burrowes Shiel (c.1874-1939). Shiel was a solicitor who had applied for a government position, namely that of Chief Crown Solicitor, in 1916, following the death of Malachy Kelly (1851-1916). The letter acknowledges Shiel's application for the Chief Crown Solicitor position.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registerede Papers; CSORP/1916/5593/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Copy 29th March, 1916 . Dear Sir, I am desired by Mr. Birrell to acknowledge the
receipt of your letter of the 27th instant applying
for the appointment as Chief Crown Solicitor for Ireland,
and to state that your application will be considered
in due course. Yours faithfully, (Sd.) A.P.M.
Under Secy.
A.P.M.
29/3
P. Burrowes Shiel, Esq., 8, Earlsfort Mansions,
DUBLIN.
